frameworks.name: metacognition
frameworks.one_line: Confidence ratings used to operationalize access to subjective experience.

frameworks.name: science of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: Methodological inspiration for measuring hard-to-pin-down subjective states.

core_question: Can metacognition and confidence ratings be used as scientific tools to study subjective affective experience in mental disorders?
key_assumptions: Subjective affective experiences are central to psychiatric symptomatology
Confidence and metacognition can be rigorously measured in humans and animals
Methods from consciousness science transfer to affective phenomena
interpretations: Operationalizing affect via metacognition could open new scientific routes for studying affective processes
This approach may inform efforts to promote psychological resilience against debilitating emotions
limitations_noted: Scientific tools for studying subjective affective states are currently limited
